---
title: "Telehealth_Policies_ITS_Analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-12"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Data Processing
### Load in libraries
```{r}
library("jsonlite")
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library("ggplot2")
library("survival")
library("survminer")
library(scales)
library(lubridate)
library(readxl)

```

### Load in Data from Excel spreadsheets
```{r}
# Import Excel file with truveta data
T_rates_df <- read_excel(
  path = "Truveta_Prescriptions.xlsx",
  sheet = "Sheet1"   
)
T_rates_df <- as.data.frame(T_rates_df)
T_rates_df <- T_rates_df %>%
  mutate(
    Date = as.Date(paste0(Date, "-01"))  # 2015-01 -> 2015-01-01
  )
T_rates_df <- T_rates_df %>%
  mutate(
    legislation_date = as.Date((legislation_date))
  )
T_rates_df <- T_rates_df %>%
  mutate(
    year = year(Date)
  )
T_rates_df$Virtual_T_Rate <- as.numeric(T_rates_df$Virtual_T_Rate)
T_rates_df$Total_T_Rate <- as.numeric(T_rates_df$Total_T_Rate)
```

## Filter years
```{r}
### Filter the dataframe to only include states that have specified number or more entries per year for the years range specified
yearly_counts <- T_rates_df %>%
  group_by(State, year) %>%
  summarise(n_entries = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(State) %>%
  complete(year = 2017:2025, fill = list(n_entries = 0)) %>%
  ungroup()

years_required <- 2017:2025


filtered_df <- T_rates_df %>%
  # 1) drop rows where Virtual_T_Rate is 0
  filter(Virtual_T_Rate != 0) %>%
  
  # 2) keep only years in desired range
  filter(year %in% years_required) %>%
  
  # 3) within each state-year, require at least 9 rows
  group_by(State, year) %>%
  filter(n() >= 9) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  
  # 4) keep only states that have *all* required years present
  group_by(State) %>%
  filter(all(years_required %in% year)) %>%
  ungroup()

unique(filtered_df$State)

```

# Individual State Analysis

## Function for running Interrupted Time Series Analysis / Segmented Regression
```{r}
run_its <- function(state_to_analyze,
                          data,
                          intervention_date = as.Date("2020-04-01")) {

  state_monthly <- data %>%
    filter(State == state_to_analyze) %>%
    arrange(Date) %>%
    mutate(
      time       = row_number(),
      post       = if_else(Date >= intervention_date, 1L, 0L),
      time_after = if_else(post == 1L,
                           time - min(time[post == 1L]) + 1L,
                           0L)
    )

  model_state <- lm(Virtual_T_Rate ~ time + post + time_after,
                    data = state_monthly)

  state_monthly <- state_monthly %>%
    mutate(fitted = fitted(model_state))

  p <- ggplot(state_monthly, aes(x = Date)) +
    geom_line(aes(y = Virtual_T_Rate), color = "black") +
    geom_line(aes(y = fitted), color = "red") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = intervention_date, linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(
      title = paste("ITS for", state_to_analyze),
      x = "Month",
      y = "Virtual T prescriptions per trans person"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()

  # return both model and plot if you like
  list(model = model_state, plot = p, data = state_monthly)
}

```

### Oregon
```{r}
mod_or <- run_its("Oregon", filtered_df)
mod_or$plot      # show plot
summary(mod_or$model)  # model output

b3_df <- data.frame(
  State = character(),
  b3    = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

b3 <- mod_or$model$coefficients[4]

b3_df <- rbind(b3_df, data.frame(
    State = "Oregon",
    b3    = b3
  )
)
```

### Louisiana
```{r}
mod_la <- run_its("Louisiana", filtered_df)
mod_la$plot      # show plot
summary(mod_la$model)  # model output

b3 <- mod_la$model$coefficients[4]

b3_df <- rbind(b3_df, data.frame(
    State = "Louisiana",
    b3    = b3
  )
)
```

### Washington
```{r}
mod_wa <- run_its("Washington", filtered_df)
mod_wa$plot      # show plot
summary(mod_wa$model)  # model output

b3 <- mod_wa$model$coefficients[4]

b3_df <- rbind(b3_df, data.frame(
    State = "Washington",
    b3    = b3
  )
)
```

### Texas
```{r}
mod_tx <- run_its("Texas", filtered_df)
mod_tx$plot      # show plot
summary(mod_tx$model)  # model output

b3 <- mod_tx$model$coefficients[4]

b3_df <- rbind(b3_df, data.frame(
    State = "Texas",
    b3    = b3
  )
)
```

### North Carolina
```{r}
mod_nc <- run_its("North Carolina", filtered_df)
mod_nc$plot      # show plot
summary(mod_nc$model)  # model output

b3 <- mod_nc$model$coefficients[4]

b3_df <- rbind(b3_df, data.frame(
    State = "North Carolina",
    b3    = b3
  )
)
```

### Wisconsin
```{r}
mod_wi <- run_its("Wisconsin", filtered_df)
mod_wi$plot      # show plot
summary(mod_wi$model)  # model output

b3 <- mod_wi$model$coefficients[4]

b3_df <- rbind(b3_df, data.frame(
    State = "Wisconsin",
    b3    = b3
  )
)
```

### Michigan
```{r}
mod_mi <- run_its("Michigan", filtered_df)
mod_mi$plot      # show plot
summary(mod_mi$model)  # model output

b3 <- mod_mi$model$coefficients[4]

b3_df <- rbind(b3_df, data.frame(
    State = "Michigan",
    b3    = b3
  )
)
```

## Plot of all B3 coefficients (Change in slope after legislation)
```{r}
max_abs <- max(abs(b3_df$b3), na.rm = TRUE)

state_colors <- c(
  "Louisiana"      = "#1b9e77",
  "North Carolina" = "#d95f02",
  "Oregon"         = "#7570b3",
  "Texas"          = "#e7298a",
  "Washington"     = "#66a61e",
  "Wisconsin"      = "#e6ab02",
  "Michigan"       = "#a6761d"
)

ggplot(b3_df, aes(x = b3, y = reorder(State, b3))) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    # extend limits a bit beyond data range to make room for labels
    limits = c(min(b3_df$b3, na.rm = TRUE) - 0.1 * max_abs,
               max(b3_df$b3, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.1 * max_abs),
    labels = label_number(style_positive = "plus")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = state_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Change in slope after interruption (b3)",
    x = "Slope change (b3)",
    y = "State"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
ggplot(b3_df, aes(x = b3, y = reorder(State, b3))) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black") +      # axis at 0
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-max_abs, max_abs)) +  # symmetric range
  labs(
    title = "Change in Slope After Legislation",
    x = "Change in Slope (Beta3 Coeff)",
    y = "State"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
